input.background: A survey selects an SRS of n=500 people from a town of 55,000. The sample shows an average of 2.30 health problems per person (standard deviation = 1.65).
input.question: Is it reasonable to assume that the sampling distribution of the mean will vary according to a Normal distribution? Explain your reasoning.

output.answer: True. Because \( n \) is large, we can count on the central limit theorem to make the sampling distribution of \( \bar{x} \) fairly Normal.
